methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring

methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Bellatrix Aerospace is a pre-Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $8M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Standard triangulation methods do not yield a definitive range. Peer-median anchoring is inapplicable: while 23 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy narrow stage and tier filters (minimum 3 required), and falling back to all-sector medians risks material misalignment. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple cannot be applied because target revenue is not on file; the sector median P/S across the 6 public comparables is 53.2x, but this requires private revenue disclosure to operationalize.

Without either sufficient narrow private peer data or disclosed revenue, this analysis cannot support a quantitative valuation range. Any forward estimate would depend critically on: (1) obtaining or inferring Bellatrix's trailing or forward revenue; (2) establishing revenue growth and margin trajectory relative to stage peers; and (3) determining appropriate revenue multiple discount relative to public comps to reflect liquidity, scale, and execution risk. A credible valuation requires one or both of these inputs. Current data suffices only to contextualize the company within the broader frontier aerospace ecosystem.
